content.principles: 1. CORS: Browser-side mechanism using HTTP headers (Access-Control-Allow-Origin) to allow cross-origin requests. Pre-flight (OPTIONS) for non-simple requests. 2. XSS: Injecting malicious scripts. Mitigation: Content Security Policy (CSP), escaping HTML, HttpOnly cookies. 3. CSRF: Forcing authenticated users to execute unwanted actions. Mitigation: Anti-CSRF tokens, SameSite cookie attribute.
pitfalls: Setting Access-Control-Allow-Origin: '*' when credentials (cookies) are required.
Trusting user-generated content without proper sanitization (XSS risk).
Exposing sensitive tokens in localStorage where XSS can steal them.
follow_up_paths: OAuth2 and JWT security best practices
HTTPS and HSTS for secure transport
